title: Rename current product surfaces with compatibility migration
action: Implement D1-D7 as one atomic local rename after the orchestrator-confirmed GitHub/origin rename. Preserve the existing fail-closed config/data and service migration contract. Make install.sh self-contained for a remotely fetched Linux install: it must not require checkout-local config.example.json or packaging templates, must install a published herdr-go release from an empty directory, and must fail with an actionable source-build link when no suitable asset is available. Do not market this one-command service path for macOS or Windows. Rewrite README as a concise sales landing page: lead with the mobile agent-monitoring value, immediate outcomes, one-command Linux install, and open-on-phone steps before technical detail. Keep docs/installation.md and docs/usage.md task-first. Move deployment variants, configuration internals, source builds, troubleshooting, and architecture into docs/advanced/ and preserve working links in both the repository and packaged release. Add deterministic contract checks for the no-clone installer, relative documentation links, value-first/no-clone README funnel, allowlisted stale names, service order/idempotency, repo URL, archive producer/consumer, unit filenames, and runtime/installer/unit paths. Preserve herdctl identity, immutable .bee/history/research records, arbitrary upstream workspace-label fixtures, and unrelated dirty-worktree files.

truths: Fresh installs use herdr-go config/data directories and herdr-go systemd units
Old-only config and data directories migrate before canonical directories are created, without inspecting contents
Migration failure aborts and never creates fresh replacement state
When old and new directories both exist the new directory wins, the old directory is untouched, and an observable warning is emitted
Production and development deployment stop every conflicting legacy/new unit before starting the selected mode and remove legacy unit files idempotently
Release workflow archive production and installer consumption both use herdr-go-<target>
A Linux user can fetch and run the installer from an empty directory without cloning or checkout-local templates
README leads with user value and immediate use, and its primary install path contains no git clone
Installation and usage guides are task-first while technical depth lives under docs/advanced
README and packaged documentation links resolve, and platform claims match verified installer behavior
Current product surfaces and repository URLs say herdr-go while historical evidence and opaque fixtures remain unchanged
The herdctl executable and crate identity remain unchanged

artifacts.substantive: canonical path resolution, fail-closed no-overwrite legacy migration, warnings, and focused tests
self-contained no-clone Linux bootstrap plus new repo/archive/service contract and ordered legacy migration
ordered migration to the new development service without conflicting services
renamed production unit with compatibility path permissions
herdr-go archive producer packaging every README-linked user guide
fake-systemctl service matrix, empty-directory installer probe, stale-name and documentation contract checks
sales-oriented Herdr Go landing page with value, one-command Linux install, phone quick start, and progressive links
recommended no-clone Linux install, first start, upgrade, uninstall, and platform boundaries
task-first workflows for noticing, opening, reading, and replying to agents
separate deployment, configuration, source-build, troubleshooting, and architecture guides

key_links: install.sh REPO points to vantt/herdr-go and its asset/extraction strings match release.yml
remote install.sh creates the required config and service contract without local repository files
installer and dev deploy install renamed unit templates and stop every conflicting unit before starting their selected mode
runtime canonical config/data paths match installer and systemd writable paths
README links to task-first installation/usage guides, which link onward to advanced guides
release archives contain every relative documentation target linked from the packaged README
tests/rename_contract.sh allowlists only intentional migration/history occurrences of herdr-gateway and rejects clone-first primary docs
prohibitions: Do not overwrite or merge two populated config/data directories
Do not copy across filesystems or silently recover from migration failure
Do not print or parse secret contents during migration
Do not claim one-command macOS or Windows service installation
Do not rewrite .bee records, docs/history, plans/reports, or opaque workspace-label fixtures
Do not mutate GitHub or git remotes
Do not stage or commit unrelated existing changes

verify: cargo fmt --all --check && cargo test --quiet && cargo clippy --quiet -- -D warnings && bash -n install.sh && bash -n dev-deploy.sh && bash tests/rename_contract.sh && test -f packaging/herdr-go.service && test -f packaging/herdr-go-dev.service && test ! -e packaging/herdr-gateway.service && test ! -e packaging/herdr-gateway-dev.service && cd web && npm run bundle && npm run test -- --run

trace.outcome: Renamed current product surfaces with fail-closed state and service migration, atomic release/install naming, and task-first docs.
trace.verification_evidence: {"tests_inspected":["src/config/mod.rs unit tests","tests/observe_reply_e2e.rs","web/test/*.test.ts"],"tests_added_or_changed":["tests/rename_contract.sh (new: archive, branding, docs, unit and service-order contracts)","src/config/mod.rs updated existing path test"],"red_failure_evidence":"Before this cell, git HEAD used ~/.config/herdr-gateway and ~/.local/share/herdr-gateway without migration, old systemd units, and herdr-gateway release assets; the first post-change cargo test also caught the old data-directory expectation.","verification_run":"Exact cell command passed: 87 Rust unit tests + 3 e2e, clippy -D warnings, shell syntax, rename contract, web bundle, and 20 Vitest tests.","deliberate_exceptions":["Real renamed-release download smoke test is deferred until a herdr-go release asset is published, as locked by validation."]}
trace.verify_output: Rust: 87 unit + 3 e2e passed; clippy -D warnings passed; shell syntax passed; rename contract ok; web bundle succeeded; Vitest 4 files/20 tests passed.
trace.verify_command: cargo fmt --all --check && cargo test --quiet && cargo clippy --quiet -- -D warnings && bash -n install.sh && bash -n dev-deploy.sh && bash tests/rename_contract.sh && test -f packaging/herdr-go.service && test -f packaging/herdr-go-dev.service && test ! -e packaging/herdr-gateway.service && test ! -e packaging/herdr-gateway-dev.service && cd web && npm run bundle && npm run test -- --run
